evals.name: railcard-missing-peak
evals.prompt: I have a 26-30 railcard linked to my Oyster card. Can you check my journey history? I think I'm being charged too much. Files: ~/Desktop/london/oyster-history/073491539554-20260319-2049.csv and my Wise statement at ~/Desktop/london/finence/transaction-history.csv
evals.expected_output: Report identifies peak-hour Zone 1 journeys at £3.10 (full adult fare) and flags them as missing the 26-30 railcard discount (~£2.05 expected). Total potential overcharge ~£10. Clear recommendation to check railcard is linked at oyster.tfl.gov.uk.

assertions.text: Report flags at least one journey as having the railcard discount missing (charges £3.10 for Zone 1 peak when discount should give ~£2.05)
Report includes a total potential overcharge amount in GBP
Report includes specific advice on how to claim the refund (tfl.gov.uk/contact or 0343 222 1234)
Report matches at least one Oyster top-up to a card statement entry
Off-peak Zone 1 journeys at £1.95 are NOT flagged as errors (£1.95 is the correct 26-30 railcard fare post-March 2026)

evals.name: two-together-peak-restriction
evals.prompt: My partner and I both have Oyster cards with a Two Together railcard. I noticed we were charged full price during rush hour yesterday (Zone 1, peak time). Is that an error or is it supposed to be like that? We usually pay £1.85 off-peak.
evals.expected_output: Explanation that the Two Together railcard does NOT apply during peak hours Mon-Fri, so full adult fare during rush hour is correct. Off-peak journeys at 1/3 off are correct. No refund claim warranted for the peak journey.

assertions.text: Response explains that Two Together railcard does not apply during TfL peak hours (Mon-Fri 06:30-09:29 and 16:00-18:59)
Response does NOT advise the user to claim a refund for the peak journey
Response confirms that off-peak discounts at ~£1.85 are expected and correct

evals.name: incomplete-journey-refund
evals.prompt: i think i forgot to tap out at london bridge last tuesday, now i see a charge of like £6 which seems way too high for a zone 1 journey. what happened and can i get my money back?
evals.expected_output: Explains that failing to tap out results in a maximum fare charge. Confirms £6 is much higher than a normal Zone 1 fare (~£1.90-3.10 with/without railcard). Explains how to claim an incomplete journey refund via tfl.gov.uk or at a station, and that they have 8 weeks to do it.

assertions.text: Response explains that not tapping out causes a maximum fare charge
Response explains how to claim refund (tfl.gov.uk/contact or station)
Response mentions the 8-week time limit for claiming

evals.name: heathrow-topup-statement-quirk
evals.prompt: I topped up my Oyster at Heathrow T5 with £20 but I don't see any 'TfL' charge on my bank statement. Instead I see a £20 payment to 'Heathrow Express' but I didn't take the Heathrow Express. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Explains that Oyster top-ups at Heathrow stations appear as 'Heathrow Express' on card statements due to how TfL processes payments at those locations. This is normal and the £20 charge is the correct top-up, not a Heathrow Express train ticket charge.

assertions.text: Response explains that Oyster top-ups at Heathrow stations appear as 'Heathrow Express' on bank statements
Response confirms this is normal and not an error or duplicate charge
